channel.title: World
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from World, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Middle East crisis live: hopes rise for renewed US-Iran talks as Tehran’s foreign minister reported to be heading to Pakistan
item.description: Iranian state media says Abbas Araqchi due to begin a trip today that includes visiting Islamabad, Muscat and Moscow The EU’s foreign chief has said t...

item.title: Justice department watchdog will investigate release of Epstein files – US politics live
item.description: An internal watchdog will review the agency’s handling of records related to Jeffrey Epstein, including whether relevant documents were properly redac...

item.title: Trump says he will ‘probably put a big tariff on the UK’ if it doesn’t drop digital services tax
item.description: President accuses Britain of trying to ‘make an easy buck’ from American tech firms, weeks after warning UK–US trade deal can be changed Business live...

item.title: Berlin culture minister resigns over irregular distribution of funds to fight antisemitism
item.description: Auditor found Sarah Wedl-Wilson approved payments of public money to groups that had not been fully vetted Berlin’s top culture official, British-born...

item.title: Assisted dying bill fails to become law after running out of time in parliament – UK politics live
item.description: Bill had successfully passed two votes in the Commons but did not face a vote in the Lords Downing Street has hit back at reports suggesting the US co...

item.title: Man given life sentence for rape and religiously aggravated assault of Sikh woman
item.description: A judge tells John Ashby, 32, that he will serve at least 14 years in prison for the attack.

item.title: The assisted dying bill has failed - but the debate isn't over
item.description: In June 2025 MPs backed the legislation but now it has run out of time to become law.

item.title: Falklands is a pressure point for the UK – and the US knows it
item.description: This story will make greater waves in the UK than in the US, which for Donald Trump is an opportunity for leverage, Joe Inwood writes.

item.title: A grim week for Starmer – but things could be about to get worse
item.description: In less than two weeks, the PM faces a crucial set of elections in Scotland, Wales and for many English councils.

item.title: No 10 says Falklands sovereignty rests with UK after report of US 'review'
item.description: An internal Pentagon document reportedly raised the prospect of a change in position in retaliation for the UK not joining the Iran war.
